title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: Simulating canonical C–C pattern — response direction tracks predictor around circle (winding number 1), modulation concentration varies cycle. Projected normal draws need rejection sampler: response literally angle noisy 2-D vector. Cartesian mean components get cyclic smooth; knots pin period (−π,π](-\pi, \pi]: fitted direction 95% band. direction depends linear predictors, band delta-method interval: joint coefficient covariance Vp pushed atan2(μ2,μ1)\mathrm{atan2}(\mu_2, \mu_1) via lpmatrix — two lines algebra, taste inference works parameters entangled:
code: library(mgcv) #> Loading required package: nlme #> This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'. library(circlss)  set.seed(20260612) n <- 300 phi <- runif(n, -pi, pi) dir_true <- phi + 0.6 * sin(phi)            # winds once per cycle gamma_true <- exp(0.6 + 0.5 * cos(phi))     # concentration scale theta <- atan2(gamma_true * sin(dir_true) + rnorm(n),                gamma_true * cos(dir_true) + rnorm(n)) dat <- data.frame(theta = theta, phi = phi) b <- gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10),                     ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10)),          family = pnlss(), data = dat, method = "REML",          knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi))) summary(b) #>  #> Family: pnlss  #> Link function: identity identity  #>  #> Formula: #> theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10) #> ~s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10) #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|) #> (Intercept)    0.05426    0.08386   0.647    0.518 #> (Intercept).1  0.01323    0.07683   0.172    0.863 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>            edf Ref.df Chi.sq p-value     #> s(phi)   6.827      8  281.6  <2e-16 *** #> s.1(phi) 6.356      8  249.8  <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 61.6% #> -REML = 289.83  Scale est. = 1         n = 300 phig <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 400) pr <- predict(b, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), type = "response") dir_hat <- atan2(pr[, 2], pr[, 1])  Xp <- predict(b, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), type = "lpmatrix") lpi <- attr(Xp, "lpi") X1 <- Xp[, lpi[[1]], drop = FALSE] X2 <- Xp[, lpi[[2]], drop = FALSE] V <- b$Vp v11 <- rowSums((X1 %*% V[lpi[[1]], lpi[[1]]]) * X1) v22 <- rowSums((X2 %*% V[lpi[[2]], lpi[[2]]]) * X2) v12 <- rowSums((X1 %*% V[lpi[[1]], lpi[[2]]]) * X2) m1 <- pr[, 1]; m2 <- pr[, 2]; r2 <- m1^2 + m2^2 se_dir <- sqrt(pmax(m2^2 * v11 + m1^2 * v22 - 2 * m1 * m2 * v12, 0)) / r2 dir_lo <- dir_hat - 1.96 * se_dir dir_hi <- dir_hat + 1.96 * se_dir

title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: axes circles, winding fit diagonal wraps: top bottom edges line, left right. Curves broken wrap avoid spurious verticals:
code: lines_wrapped <- function(x, y, ...) {   y <- atan2(sin(y), cos(y))   br <- which(abs(diff(y)) > pi)   s0 <- c(1, br + 1); s1 <- c(br, length(y))   for (k in seq_along(s0))     if (s1[k] > s0[k]) lines(x[s0[k]:s1[k]], y[s0[k]:s1[k]], ...) }  op <- par(mar = c(4, 4, 1, 1)) plot(dat$phi, dat$theta, pch = 19, col = adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", 0.5),      cex = 0.6, xlab = expression(varphi ~ "(predictor angle)"),      ylab = expression(theta ~ "(response angle)"),      xlim = c(-pi, pi), ylim = c(-pi, pi), axes = FALSE) axis(1, at = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),      labels = expression(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi)) axis(2, at = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),      labels = expression(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi)) box() lines_wrapped(phig, dir_true_g <- phig + 0.6 * sin(phig),               col = "gray60", lwd = 1.6, lty = 2) lines_wrapped(phig, dir_lo, col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", 0.5), lwd = 1) lines_wrapped(phig, dir_hi, col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", 0.5), lwd = 1) lines_wrapped(phig, dir_hat, col = "#c0392b", lwd = 2.6) legend("topleft", bty = "n", lwd = c(2.6, 1.6), lty = c(1, 2),        col = c("#c0392b", "gray60"), bg = NA,        legend = c(expression(hat(mu)(varphi) %+-% "95% band"), "truth")) par(op)

title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: (φ,θ)(\varphi, \theta) maps ((R+rcos⁡θ)cos⁡φ,(R+rcos⁡θ)sin⁡φ,rsin⁡θ)\big((R + r\cos\theta)\cos\varphi,\ (R + r\cos\theta)\sin\varphi,\ r\sin\theta\big). Base graphics enough: persp() supplies projection matrix, everything drawn trans3d(), depth-based fading 3-D cue 95% band translucent ribbon (dashed circle outer equator θ=0\theta = 0):  fitted mean direction winds around tube going around ring — (1,1) curve torus, delta-method ribbon hugging . shape tan-half family produce.
code: torus_xyz <- function(phi, theta, R = 1.9, r = 0.95) {   list(x = (R + r * cos(theta)) * cos(phi),        y = (R + r * cos(theta)) * sin(phi),        z = r * sin(theta)) }  # view-space depth from the persp transformation matrix (trans3d gives # only the projected x/y; the z/w component orders front vs back) depth3d <- function(x, y, z, pm) {   p <- cbind(x, y, z, 1) %*% pm   p[, 3] / p[, 4] }  draw_torus <- function(dat, mu_hat, phig, lo = NULL, hi = NULL,                        R = 1.9, r = 0.95, theta_view = 45, phi_view = 38) {   op <- par(mar = c(0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 0.2))   on.exit(par(op))   lim <- R + r + 0.1   pm <- persp(x = c(-lim, lim), y = c(-lim, lim),               z = matrix(c(-r, -r, r, r), 2, 2),               zlim = c(-r - 0.5, r + 0.5),               theta = theta_view, phi = phi_view, d = 4,               scale = FALSE, expand = 1,               col = NA, border = NA, box = FALSE, axes = FALSE)    ## wireframe: tube circles at fixed phi, ring circles at fixed theta   thd <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 80)   for (p in seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 49)[-1]) {     w <- torus_xyz(rep(p, length(thd)), thd, R, r)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }   phd <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 160)   for (t in seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 25)[-1]) {     w <- torus_xyz(phd, rep(t, length(phd)), R, r)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }    ## outer equator (theta = 0): the zero-response reference line   w <- torus_xyz(phd, rep(0, length(phd)), R, r)   lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray55", lty = 3, lwd = 0.9)    ## data on the torus surface, depth-faded (near = opaque, far = faint)   w <- torus_xyz(dat$phi, dat$theta, R, r)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.15 + 0.7 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   pt <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   ord <- order(dp)  # paint far points first   points(pt$x[ord], pt$y[ord], pch = 19,          cex = 0.4 + 0.25 * a[ord],          col = sapply(a[ord], function(ai) adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", ai)))    ## 95% ribbon between lo(phi) and hi(phi): translucent quads painted in   ## depth order, subdivided across the band so it follows the tube   if (!is.null(lo) && !is.null(hi)) {     K <- 4     i0 <- seq_len(length(phig) - 1)     quads <- list()     for (k in seq_len(K)) {       t0 <- lo + (hi - lo) * (k - 1) / K       t1 <- lo + (hi - lo) * k / K       for (i in i0) {         th <- c(t0[i], t0[i + 1], t1[i + 1], t1[i])         ph <- c(phig[i], phig[i + 1], phig[i + 1], phig[i])         w <- torus_xyz(ph, th, R, r)         quads[[length(quads) + 1]] <-           list(p = trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm),                d = mean(depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)))       }     }     dq <- vapply(quads, `[[`, numeric(1), "d")     aq <- 0.05 + 0.13 * (dq - min(dq)) / diff(range(dq))     for (j in order(dq)) {       polygon(quads[[j]]$p$x, quads[[j]]$p$y, border = NA,               col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", aq[j]))     }   }    ## fitted mean-direction curve, painted in depth order, far half faint   w <- torus_xyz(phig, mu_hat, R, r)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   cv <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.25 + 0.75 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   seg <- data.frame(x0 = head(cv$x, -1), y0 = head(cv$y, -1),                     x1 = tail(cv$x, -1), y1 = tail(cv$y, -1),                     d = head(dp, -1), a = head(a, -1))   seg <- seg[order(seg$d), ]   segments(seg$x0, seg$y0, seg$x1, seg$y1,            col = sapply(seg$a, function(ai) adjustcolor("#c0392b", ai)),            lwd = 2.2 + 1.6 * seg$a, lend = 1)   invisible(pm) } draw_torus(dat, dir_hat, phig, lo = dir_lo, hi = dir_hi)

title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: relationship oscillates rather winds — response swinging around reference direction predictor cycles (diurnal wind rotation, heading vs season) — vmlss buys something pnlss : mean direction μ(φ)\mu(\varphi) concentration κ(φ)\kappa(\varphi) separate, interpretable parameters, smooth credible band. two estimated smooths link scale — η1\eta_1 drives tan-half location, η2\eta_2 log concentration; per-parameter readout point family:  mean direction’s 95% band comes link-scale standard errors pushed monotone tan-half link (standard endpoint transform), fit goes torus:  oscillation: curve rises tube response leads, dips lags, crosses dashed zero-equator twice — winding number zero, exactly class vmlss can represent. scope rule one breath: tan-half map keeps μ\mu inside (−π,π)(-\pi, \pi) — can neither cross antipode response origin wind, rotation-type data reach pnlss (model wrapped residual θ−mφ\theta - m\varphi known winding mm add mφm\varphi back).
code: rvm <- function(n, mu, kappa) {   mu <- rep_len(mu, n); kappa <- rep_len(kappa, n); out <- numeric(n)   for (i in seq_len(n)) {     k <- kappa[i]     a <- 1 + sqrt(1 + 4 * k * k); b <- (a - sqrt(2 * a)) / (2 * k)     r <- (1 + b * b) / (2 * b)     repeat {       z <- cos(pi * runif(1)); f <- (1 + r * z) / (r + z)       cc <- k * (r - f); u2 <- runif(1)       if (cc * (2 - cc) - u2 > 0 || log(cc / u2) + 1 - cc >= 0) {         out[i] <- sign(runif(1) - 0.5) * acos(max(min(f, 1), -1)) + mu[i]         break       }     }   }   atan2(sin(out), cos(out)) }  set.seed(20260612) n <- 200 phi2 <- runif(n, -pi, pi) theta2 <- rvm(n, 2 * atan(1.6 * sin(phi2)), exp(1.4 + 0.6 * cos(phi2))) dat2 <- data.frame(theta = theta2, phi = phi2)  b2 <- gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10),                      ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10)),           family = vmlss(), data = dat2, method = "REML",           knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi))) plot(b2, pages = 1, scheme = 1) prl <- predict(b2, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), type = "link",                se.fit = TRUE) mu_hat <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1]) mu_lo <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1] - 1.96 * prl$se.fit[, 1]) mu_hi <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1] + 1.96 * prl$se.fit[, 1]) draw_torus(dat2, mu_hat, phig, lo = mu_lo, hi = mu_hi)

what: Same models, same numbers, in Python
title: Circular–circular regression on the torus
text: families mgcv twins pycircstat2’s CLRegression(..., family=pnlss | vmlss), differentially tested every release — identical data, Newton-REML sides. cyclic-spline cases, including winding projected-normal one, agree near machine precision (see tests/testthat/test-*-parity.R dev/parity/ repository).

title: Circular–linear regression on the cylinder
text: mean direction swings around reference xx moves, concentration varies : two estimated smooths link scale — per-parameter readout (location concentration, credible band) distributional-regression payoff:
code: library(mgcv) #> Loading required package: nlme #> This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'. library(circlss)  rvm <- function(n, mu, kappa) {   mu <- rep_len(mu, n); kappa <- rep_len(kappa, n); out <- numeric(n)   for (i in seq_len(n)) {     k <- kappa[i]     a <- 1 + sqrt(1 + 4 * k * k); b <- (a - sqrt(2 * a)) / (2 * k)     r <- (1 + b * b) / (2 * b)     repeat {       z <- cos(pi * runif(1)); f <- (1 + r * z) / (r + z)       cc <- k * (r - f); u2 <- runif(1)       if (cc * (2 - cc) - u2 > 0 || log(cc / u2) + 1 - cc >= 0) {         out[i] <- sign(runif(1) - 0.5) * acos(max(min(f, 1), -1)) + mu[i]         break       }     }   }   atan2(sin(out), cos(out)) }  set.seed(20260612) n <- 250 x <- runif(n) mu_true <- 2 * atan(1.6 * sin(2 * pi * x)) kappa_true <- exp(1.3 + 0.9 * cos(2 * pi * x)) dat <- data.frame(theta = rvm(n, mu_true, kappa_true), x = x)  b <- gam(list(theta ~ s(x), ~ s(x)),          family = vmlss(), data = dat, method = "REML") summary(b) #>  #> Family: vmlss  #> Link function: tanhalf log  #>  #> Formula: #> theta ~ s(x) #> ~s(x) #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)    0.02990    0.04863   0.615    0.539     #> (Intercept).1  1.31958    0.08459  15.600   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>          edf Ref.df Chi.sq p-value     #> s(x)   7.110  8.085 780.44  <2e-16 *** #> s.1(x) 4.608  5.631  79.98  <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 63.4% #> -REML = 247.47  Scale est. = 1         n = 250 plot(b, pages = 1, scheme = 1)

title: Circular–linear regression on the cylinder
text: 95% band μ(x)\mu(x) link-scale interval pushed monotone tan-half link:  Remember vertical axis circle — top bottom edges line. cylinder shows without cut.
code: xg <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 400) prl <- predict(b, newdata = data.frame(x = xg), type = "link",                se.fit = TRUE) mu_hat <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1]) mu_lo <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1] - 1.96 * prl$se.fit[, 1]) mu_hi <- 2 * atan(prl$fit[, 1] + 1.96 * prl$se.fit[, 1])  op <- par(mar = c(4, 4, 1, 1)) plot(dat$x, dat$theta, pch = 19, col = adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", 0.5),      cex = 0.6, xlab = "x (linear covariate)",      ylab = expression(theta ~ "(response angle)"),      ylim = c(-pi, pi), axes = FALSE) axis(1) axis(2, at = c(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi),      labels = expression(-pi, -pi/2, 0, pi/2, pi)) box() polygon(c(xg, rev(xg)), c(mu_lo, rev(mu_hi)), border = NA,         col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", 0.15)) lines(xg, 2 * atan(1.6 * sin(2 * pi * xg)), col = "gray60",       lwd = 1.6, lty = 2) lines(xg, mu_hat, col = "#c0392b", lwd = 2.6) legend("topright", bty = "n", lwd = c(2.6, 1.6), lty = c(1, 2),        col = c("#c0392b", "gray60"),        legend = c(expression(hat(mu)(x) %+-% "95% band"), "truth")) par(op)

title: Circular–linear regression on the cylinder
text: (x,θ)(x, \theta) maps ((x),rcos⁡θ,rsin⁡θ)\big((x),\ r\cos\theta,\ r\sin\theta\big) covariate stretched along axis. toolkit torus article: persp() projection matrix, trans3d() drawing, depth-based fading, 95% band translucent ribbon (dashed axial line θ=0\theta = 0, reference direction):  fitted mean direction swings around reference line back — never wraps tube, exactly class tan-half link can represent.
code: cyl_xyz <- function(x, theta, L = 1.7, r = 1.05) {   list(x = L * (2 * x - 1),  # covariate [0, 1] -> [-L, L] along the axis        y = r * cos(theta),        z = r * sin(theta)) }  # view-space depth from the persp transformation matrix depth3d <- function(x, y, z, pm) {   p <- cbind(x, y, z, 1) %*% pm   p[, 3] / p[, 4] }  draw_cylinder <- function(dat, mu_hat, xg, lo = NULL, hi = NULL,                           L = 1.7, r = 1.05,                           theta_view = 30, phi_view = 28) {   op <- par(mar = c(0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 0.2))   on.exit(par(op))   pm <- persp(x = c(-L - 0.15, L + 0.15), y = c(-r - 0.2, r + 0.2),               z = matrix(c(-r, -r, r, r), 2, 2),               zlim = c(-r - 0.4, r + 0.4),               theta = theta_view, phi = phi_view, d = 4,               scale = FALSE, expand = 1,               col = NA, border = NA, box = FALSE, axes = FALSE)    ## wireframe: rings at fixed x, axial lines at fixed theta   thd <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 80)   for (p in seq(0, 1, length.out = 18)) {     w <- cyl_xyz(rep(p, length(thd)), thd, L, r)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }   xd <- seq(0, 1, length.out = 120)   for (t in seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 25)[-1]) {     w <- cyl_xyz(xd, rep(t, length(xd)), L, r)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }    ## theta = 0: the reference-direction line along the axis   w <- cyl_xyz(xd, rep(0, length(xd)), L, r)   lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray55", lty = 3, lwd = 0.9)    ## data on the surface, depth-faded   w <- cyl_xyz(dat$x, dat$theta, L, r)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.15 + 0.7 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   pt <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   ord <- order(dp)   points(pt$x[ord], pt$y[ord], pch = 19,          cex = 0.4 + 0.25 * a[ord],          col = sapply(a[ord], function(ai) adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", ai)))    ## 95% ribbon, depth-ordered translucent quads   if (!is.null(lo) && !is.null(hi)) {     K <- 4     i0 <- seq_len(length(xg) - 1)     quads <- list()     for (k in seq_len(K)) {       t0 <- lo + (hi - lo) * (k - 1) / K       t1 <- lo + (hi - lo) * k / K       for (i in i0) {         th <- c(t0[i], t0[i + 1], t1[i + 1], t1[i])         ph <- c(xg[i], xg[i + 1], xg[i + 1], xg[i])         w <- cyl_xyz(ph, th, L, r)         quads[[length(quads) + 1]] <-           list(p = trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm),                d = mean(depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)))       }     }     dq <- vapply(quads, `[[`, numeric(1), "d")     aq <- 0.05 + 0.13 * (dq - min(dq)) / diff(range(dq))     for (j in order(dq)) {       polygon(quads[[j]]$p$x, quads[[j]]$p$y, border = NA,               col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", aq[j]))     }   }    ## fitted mean-direction curve, depth-painted   w <- cyl_xyz(xg, mu_hat, L, r)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   cv <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.25 + 0.75 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   seg <- data.frame(x0 = head(cv$x, -1), y0 = head(cv$y, -1),                     x1 = tail(cv$x, -1), y1 = tail(cv$y, -1),                     d = head(dp, -1), a = head(a, -1))   seg <- seg[order(seg$d), ]   segments(seg$x0, seg$y0, seg$x1, seg$y1,            col = sapply(seg$a, function(ai) adjustcolor("#c0392b", ai)),            lwd = 2.2 + 1.6 * seg$a, lend = 1)   invisible(pm) } draw_cylinder(dat, mu_hat, xg, lo = mu_lo, hi = mu_hi)

what: Drifting phase: the helix, with pnlss
title: Circular–linear regression on the cylinder
text: response keeps rotating xx grows — phase advances, θ≈ωx\theta \approx \omega x — mean must cross ±π\pm\pi wrap, vmlss branch. pnlss represents two ordinary smooths, μ1(x)\mu_1(x) μ2(x)\mu_2(x), whose angle winds freely:  helix: fitted direction wraps cylinder one half times x=0x = 0 x=1x = 1, delta-method ribbon along ride. trade torus applies — pnlss buys unbounded rotation price entangling direction concentration (μ1,μ2)(\mu_1, \mu_2).
code: set.seed(20260612) n <- 300 x2 <- runif(n) dir_true <- 3 * pi * x2 - pi / 2 + 0.4 * sin(2 * pi * x2)  # 1.5 turns gamma_true <- exp(0.7 + 0.3 * cos(2 * pi * x2)) theta2 <- atan2(gamma_true * sin(dir_true) + rnorm(n),                 gamma_true * cos(dir_true) + rnorm(n)) dat2 <- data.frame(theta = theta2, x = x2)  b2 <- gam(list(theta ~ s(x, k = 12), ~ s(x, k = 12)),           family = pnlss(), data = dat2, method = "REML")  pr2 <- predict(b2, newdata = data.frame(x = xg), type = "response") dir_hat <- atan2(pr2[, 2], pr2[, 1])  # delta-method band: joint Vp through atan2(mu2, mu1) Xp <- predict(b2, newdata = data.frame(x = xg), type = "lpmatrix") lpi <- attr(Xp, "lpi") X1 <- Xp[, lpi[[1]], drop = FALSE] X2 <- Xp[, lpi[[2]], drop = FALSE] V <- b2$Vp v11 <- rowSums((X1 %*% V[lpi[[1]], lpi[[1]]]) * X1) v22 <- rowSums((X2 %*% V[lpi[[2]], lpi[[2]]]) * X2) v12 <- rowSums((X1 %*% V[lpi[[1]], lpi[[2]]]) * X2) m1 <- pr2[, 1]; m2 <- pr2[, 2]; r2 <- m1^2 + m2^2 se_dir <- sqrt(pmax(m2^2 * v11 + m1^2 * v22 - 2 * m1 * m2 * v12, 0)) / r2  draw_cylinder(dat2, dir_hat, xg, phi_view = 32,               lo = dir_hat - 1.96 * se_dir, hi = dir_hat + 1.96 * se_dir)

what: Same models, same numbers, in Python
title: Circular–linear regression on the cylinder
text: fits mgcv twins pycircstat2’s CLRegression(..., family=vmlss | pnlss) differentially tested every release — identical data, Newton-REML sides (see tests/testthat/test-*-parity.R dev/parity/ repository).

title: Linear–circular regression on the cylinder
text: (gaulss models mean precision τ≈1/σ\tau \approx 1/\sigma; second fitted column τ\tau, σ̂=1/τ̂\hat\sigma = 1/\hat\tau.)
code: library(mgcv) #> Loading required package: nlme #> This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'.  set.seed(20260612) n <- 300 phi <- runif(n, -pi, pi) m_true <- 2 + 1.5 * sin(phi) + 0.8 * cos(2 * phi) sd_true <- exp(-0.5 + 0.6 * cos(phi)) dat <- data.frame(y = rnorm(n, m_true, sd_true), phi = phi)  b <- gam(list(y ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10),                 ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10)),          family = gaulss(), data = dat, method = "REML",          knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi))) summary(b) #>  #> Family: gaulss  #> Link function: identity logb  #>  #> Formula: #> y ~ s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10) #> ~s(phi, bs = "cc", k = 10) #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)    2.02300    0.03881   52.13   <2e-16 *** #> (Intercept).1 -0.59110    0.04174  -14.16   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>            edf Ref.df Chi.sq p-value     #> s(phi)   7.144      8 1604.6  <2e-16 *** #> s.1(phi) 3.954      8  119.4  <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 84.5% #> -REML = 284.64  Scale est. = 1         n = 300

title: Linear–circular regression on the cylinder
text: Mean curve 95% band, plus heteroscedasticity readout σ̂(φ)\hat\sigma(\varphi) — periodic, closing seamlessly ±π\pm\pi:
code: phig <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 400) pr <- predict(b, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig), se.fit = TRUE) m_hat <- pr$fit[, 1] m_lo <- m_hat - 1.96 * pr$se.fit[, 1] m_hi <- m_hat + 1.96 * pr$se.fit[, 1] sd_hat <- 1 / predict(b, newdata = data.frame(phi = phig),                       type = "response")[, 2]  op <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2), mar = c(4, 4, 1.5, 1)) plot(dat$phi, dat$y, pch = 19, col = adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", 0.45),      cex = 0.6, xlab = expression(varphi), ylab = "y",      main = "mean", axes = FALSE) axis(1, at = c(-pi, 0, pi), labels = expression(-pi, 0, pi)) axis(2) box() polygon(c(phig, rev(phig)), c(m_lo, rev(m_hi)), border = NA,         col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", 0.15)) lines(phig, 2 + 1.5 * sin(phig) + 0.8 * cos(2 * phig),       col = "gray60", lwd = 1.6, lty = 2) lines(phig, m_hat, col = "#c0392b", lwd = 2.4)  plot(phig, sd_hat, type = "l", col = "#c0392b", lwd = 2.4,      xlab = expression(varphi), ylab = expression(hat(sigma)(varphi)),      main = "scale", axes = FALSE, ylim = range(0, sd_hat, exp(0.1))) axis(1, at = c(-pi, 0, pi), labels = expression(-pi, 0, pi)) axis(2) box() lines(phig, exp(-0.5 + 0.6 * cos(phig)), col = "gray60",       lwd = 1.6, lty = 2) par(op)

title: Linear–circular regression on the cylinder
text: L–C cylinder stands upright: circular covariate runs around can, response height. (φ,y)(\varphi, y) maps (rcos⁡φ,rsin⁡φ,h(y))\big(r\cos\varphi,\ r\sin\varphi,\ h(y)\big), fitted mean closed loop around can varying height, ribbon. toolkit torus: persp() projection, trans3d() drawing, depth-fading 3-D cue (dashed ring sits overall mean height):  loop closes — cyclic basis job; unconstrained s(phi) let two ends cycle disagree.
code: # view-space depth from the persp transformation matrix depth3d <- function(x, y, z, pm) {   p <- cbind(x, y, z, 1) %*% pm   p[, 3] / p[, 4] }  draw_can <- function(dat, m_hat, phig, lo = NULL, hi = NULL,                      r = 1, H = 1.5, theta_view = 30, phi_view = 16) {   yl <- range(dat$y, lo, hi)   can_xyz <- function(phi, y) {     h <- (y - yl[1]) / diff(yl) * 2 * H - H     list(x = r * cos(phi), y = r * sin(phi), z = h)   }   op <- par(mar = c(0.2, 0.2, 0.2, 0.2))   on.exit(par(op))   pm <- persp(x = c(-r - 0.3, r + 0.3), y = c(-r - 0.3, r + 0.3),               z = matrix(c(-H, -H, H, H), 2, 2),               zlim = c(-H - 0.3, H + 0.3),               theta = theta_view, phi = phi_view, d = 4,               scale = FALSE, expand = 1,               col = NA, border = NA, box = FALSE, axes = FALSE)    ## wireframe: vertical generators + horizontal rings   hd <- seq(yl[1], yl[2], length.out = 40)   for (p in seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 49)[-1]) {     w <- can_xyz(rep(p, length(hd)), hd)     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }   phd <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 160)   for (t in seq(yl[1], yl[2], length.out = 13)) {     w <- can_xyz(phd, rep(t, length(phd)))     lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray88", lwd = 0.6)   }    ## reference ring at the overall mean height   w <- can_xyz(phd, rep(mean(dat$y), length(phd)))   lines(trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm), col = "gray55", lty = 3, lwd = 0.9)    ## data on the surface, depth-faded   w <- can_xyz(dat$phi, dat$y)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.15 + 0.7 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   pt <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   ord <- order(dp)   points(pt$x[ord], pt$y[ord], pch = 19,          cex = 0.4 + 0.25 * a[ord],          col = sapply(a[ord], function(ai) adjustcolor("#2c5aa0", ai)))    ## 95% ribbon, depth-ordered translucent quads   if (!is.null(lo) && !is.null(hi)) {     K <- 4     i0 <- seq_len(length(phig) - 1)     quads <- list()     for (k in seq_len(K)) {       t0 <- lo + (hi - lo) * (k - 1) / K       t1 <- lo + (hi - lo) * k / K       for (i in i0) {         hh <- c(t0[i], t0[i + 1], t1[i + 1], t1[i])         pp <- c(phig[i], phig[i + 1], phig[i + 1], phig[i])         w <- can_xyz(pp, hh)         quads[[length(quads) + 1]] <-           list(p = trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm),                d = mean(depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)))       }     }     dq <- vapply(quads, `[[`, numeric(1), "d")     aq <- 0.05 + 0.13 * (dq - min(dq)) / diff(range(dq))     for (j in order(dq)) {       polygon(quads[[j]]$p$x, quads[[j]]$p$y, border = NA,               col = adjustcolor("#c0392b", aq[j]))     }   }    ## fitted mean curve: a closed loop around the can, depth-painted   w <- can_xyz(phig, m_hat)   dp <- depth3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   cv <- trans3d(w$x, w$y, w$z, pm)   a <- 0.25 + 0.75 * (dp - min(dp)) / diff(range(dp))   seg <- data.frame(x0 = head(cv$x, -1), y0 = head(cv$y, -1),                     x1 = tail(cv$x, -1), y1 = tail(cv$y, -1),                     d = head(dp, -1), a = head(a, -1))   seg <- seg[order(seg$d), ]   segments(seg$x0, seg$y0, seg$x1, seg$y1,            col = sapply(seg$a, function(ai) adjustcolor("#c0392b", ai)),            lwd = 2.2 + 1.6 * seg$a, lend = 1)   invisible(pm) } draw_can(dat, m_hat, phig, lo = m_lo, hi = m_hi)

title: Linear–circular regression on the cylinder
text: Flip roles — circular response — leave plain mgcv ships: exactly gap circlss fills, vmlss() pnlss() general families whose every parameter gets smooth. See C–L article linear covariate case (helix needs pnlss), C–C article doubly circular case torus.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ziwei Huang. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Huang Z (2026). circlss: Circular-Response Location-Scale Families 'mgcv'. R package version 0.0.2, https://huangziwei.github.io/circlss/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {circlss: Circular-Response Location-Scale Families for 'mgcv'},   author = {Ziwei Huang},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.2},   url = {https://huangziwei.github.io/circlss/}, }

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: Circular-response location-scale families mgcv’s GAM engine: fit penalized-spline regression models response angle, every distribution parameter getting smooth.  Circular–circular regression natural canvas: predictor angle around ring, response angle around tube, fitted mean direction (red, 95% delta-method ribbon) winding around torus — gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs="cc"), ~ s(phi, bs="cc")), family = pnlss()). Code torus article. Documentation: https://huangziwei.github.io/circlss/ — worked examples three response–predictor geometries: circular–linear (cylinder), linear–circular (cylinder, mgcv-native), circular–circular (torus). Two families far: von Mises vmlss() (tan-half location link, log concentration — reference implementation) projected normal pnlss() (identity-linked Cartesian mean components — branch cut, fitted direction can sweep wind around full circle, natural choice circular–circular regression). remaining circular families (wrapped Cauchy, Jones–Pewsey, sine-skewed JP, cardioid, Cartwright, wrapped normal, Kato–Jones) follow v0.1.0, ported pycircstat2 recipe.

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: Requires mgcv >= 1.9.4 (R >= 4.5 recommended).
code: # released versions: not yet on CRAN; install from GitHub remotes::install_github("huangziwei/circlss")  # or from a local checkout install.packages("path/to/circlss", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: families mgcv general families: pass gam() list two formulas, one per distribution parameter — vmlss() mean direction (Fisher–Lee tan-half link) log concentration; pnlss() two Cartesian components mean vector. circular–circular data prefer pnlss(): tan-half map vmlss() wind (winding number zero) cross antipode response origin, circular covariate suits oscillation around reference direction, rotation-type association — torus article shows . response radians, branch ([0, 2pi) (-pi, pi]). Log-likelihood derivatives implemented fourth order, full Newton REML works (optimizer = "efs" general-family criteria); everything mgcv provides general families — summary, plot, predict, gam.check, AIC, smooth selection, tensor smooths, random effects — comes free.
code: library(mgcv) library(circlss)  # circular response, linear covariate -- distributional von Mises: # mean direction and log-concentration each get their own smooth b <- gam(list(theta ~ s(x),    # location, tan-half link                     ~ s(x)),   # log concentration          family = vmlss(), data = dat, method = "REML")  summary(b) plot(b, pages = 1) predict(b, newdata = nd, type = "response")  # columns: mu (radians), kappa  # circular-circular regression -- projected normal: the fitted direction # atan2(mu2, mu1) has no branch cut and can wind around the circle b2 <- gam(list(theta ~ s(phi, bs = "cc"), ~ s(phi, bs = "cc")),           family = pnlss(), data = dat, method = "REML",           knots = list(phi = c(-pi, pi))) fv <- fitted(b2)                      # columns: mu1, mu2 direction <- atan2(fv[, 2], fv[, 1])  # fitted mean direction

title: circlss — circular-response families for mgcv
text: derivative algebra transcribed pycircstat2’s FD-verified implementations, every release differentially tested : models data (file-exchanged, Newton-REML sides) must agree within pinned tolerances. v0.0.1, battery agrees machine precision (~1e-11) parametric cyclic-spline models, ~3e-5 (smoothing parameters, log-likelihood) fitted curves within 2e-6 radians thin-plate models, basis eigen-decomposition makes bitwise agreement impossible. REML criteria match ~1e-9 normalizing conventions (hea reports -2 log REML, mgcv -log REML). dev/parity/run.sh reruns whole side--side comparison; frozen battery asserted hermetically tests/testthat/test-vmlss-parity.R.

what: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: general family implementing distributional regression circular (angular) response \(y\) radians projected normal law: \(y\) angle bivariate normal mean \((\mu_1, \mu_2)\) identity covariance. mean component gets linear predictor identity link, mgcv::gam called list two formulas: first names response models \(\mu_1\), second models \(\mu_2\).

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: link Two-element list link names two Cartesian mean components. "identity" available .

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: object class c("general.family", "extended.family", "family") use gam.

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: fitted mean direction \(\mathrm{atan2}(\mu_2, \mu_1)\) implied concentration grows \(\|\mu\|\). direction assembled two unconstrained components, link branch cut: unlike tan-half parameterization vmlss, fitted mean direction can cross angle can wind around circle (e.g. \(\mu(\varphi) = \varphi\) cyclic covariate), makes pnlss natural family circular-circular regression rotation-type association. trade-interpretability: location concentration entangled \((\mu_1, \mu_2)\) rather separated distinct parameters. Fitted values predict(..., type = "response") return two Cartesian components \((\mu_1, \mu_2)\) columns, matching identity links; compute direction atan2(fit[, 2], fit[, 1]) concentration scale sqrt(rowSums(fit^2)). Log-likelihood derivatives implemented fourth order (full Newton method = "REML", optimizer = "efs"). derivative algebra transcribed finite-difference-verified implementations Python package pycircstat2, fitted results differentially tested . "pearson" residuals alias "deviance" (signed root twice log-likelihood gap fitted-direction mode), pycircstat2.

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
text: Presnell, B., Morrison, S. P. Littell, R. C. (1998) Projected multivariate linear models directional data. Journal American Statistical Association 93, 1068-1077. Wood, S. N., Pya, N. Saefken, B. (2016) Smoothing parameter model selection general smooth models. Journal American Statistical Association 111, 1548-1575.

title: Projected normal location family for mgcv GAMs — pnlss
code: library(mgcv) #> Loading required package: nlme #> This is mgcv 1.9-4. For overview type '?mgcv'. set.seed(1) n <- 300 x <- runif(n) m1 <- 1.5 * sin(2 * pi * x) + 0.5 m2 <- 1.2 * cos(2 * pi * x) y <- atan2(m2 + rnorm(n), m1 + rnorm(n))  # exact projected normal draws b <- gam(list(y ~ s(x), ~ s(x)), family = pnlss(), method = "REML") summary(b) #>  #> Family: pnlss  #> Link function: identity identity  #>  #> Formula: #> y ~ s(x) #> <environment: 0x559c6eae26d0> #> ~s(x) #> <environment: 0x559c6eae26d0> #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)    0.51074    0.07397   6.905 5.03e-12 *** #> (Intercept).1 -0.05383    0.06932  -0.777    0.437     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>          edf Ref.df Chi.sq p-value     #> s(x)   5.455  6.598  200.3  <2e-16 *** #> s.1(x) 4.522  5.566  146.2  <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 47.2% #> -REML =  383.6  Scale est. = 1         n = 300 fv <- fitted(b) direction <- atan2(fv[, 2], fv[, 1])

what: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: general family implementing distributional regression circular (angular) response \(y\) radians, $$y \sim \mathrm{vM}(\mu, \kappa),$$ mean direction \(\mu\) (log) concentration \(\kappa\) get linear predictor, may contain smooth terms. Used mgcv::gam list two formulas: first specifies response model \(\mu\), second model \(\log\kappa\).

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: link Two-element list link names, mean direction concentration. Currently defaults available: "tanhalf" location "log" concentration.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: object class c("general.family", "extended.family", "family") use gam.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: mean direction uses Fisher-Lee tan-half link, \(\mu = 2\arctan(\eta) \(-\pi, \pi)\): linear predictor's intercept sets reference direction antipode direction unrepresentable, standard caveat link. concentration uses log link. Log-likelihood derivatives fourth order implemented, family supports full Newton smoothing-parameter estimation method = "REML" (general-family criteria, including optimizer = "efs"). derivative implementations transcriptions finite-difference verified implementations Python package pycircstat2, fitted results differentially tested . response supplied radians; branch (example \([0, 2\pi)\) \((-\pi, \pi]\)) acceptable since density periodic. Fitted values two-column matrix: mean direction \((-\pi, \pi)\) concentration.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
text: Fisher, N. . Lee, . J. (1992) Regression models angular response. Biometrics 48, 665-677. Wood, S. N., Pya, N. Saefken, B. (2016) Smoothing parameter model selection general smooth models. Journal American Statistical Association 111, 1548-1575.

title: von Mises location-scale family for mgcv GAMs — vmlss
code: library(mgcv) set.seed(1) n <- 300 x <- runif(n) mu <- 2 * atan(1.5 * sin(2 * pi * x)) kappa <- exp(1 + 0.8 * cos(2 * pi * x)) ## von Mises deviates via wrapped rejection-free approximation for the ## example only: use circular::rvonmises or the family's rd in practice y <- mu + rnorm(n) / sqrt(kappa)  # high-kappa approximation, example only b <- gam(list(y ~ s(x), ~ s(x)), family = vmlss(), method = "REML") summary(b) #>  #> Family: vmlss  #> Link function: tanhalf log  #>  #> Formula: #> y ~ s(x) #> <environment: 0x559c74f4d588> #> ~s(x) #> <environment: 0x559c74f4d588> #>  #> Parametric coefficients: #>               Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)    0.10346    0.04183   2.474   0.0134 *   #> (Intercept).1  1.16066    0.07234  16.045   <2e-16 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Approximate significance of smooth terms: #>         edf Ref.df Chi.sq  p-value     #> s(x)   6.91  7.962 669.76  < 2e-16 *** #> s.1(x) 2.87  3.569  19.84 0.000442 *** #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Deviance explained = 60.7% #> -REML = 312.05  Scale est. = 1         n = 300 plot(b, pages = 1)

title: circlss 0.0.2
text: pnlss(): projected normal family — response angle bivariate normal mean (mu1, mu2) identity covariance; Cartesian mean components carry identity links linear predictors. tan-half branch cut: fitted mean direction can cross angle can wind around circle, making pnlss natural family circular–circular regression rotation-type association (theta ~ phi). Full fourth-order derivatives (Newton REML EFS). differential battery pycircstat2 doubles eight cases: four pnlss cases (parametric, thin plate, cyclic--winding, small-n) agree tolerance classes vmlss — near machine precision deterministic bases (winding cyclic case ~1e-10), optimizer-stopping tolerance thin-plate ones. TIGHT class’s coefficient/curve thresholds recalibrated 1e-9 1e-7 cover flatter parametric projected-normal likelihood (log-likelihood still agrees 1e-13 ).

title: circlss 0.0.1
text: Initial release. vmlss(): von Mises location-scale general family mgcv::gam() — Fisher–Lee tan-half link mean direction log link concentration, parameter linear predictor smooths. Log-likelihood derivatives fourth order, full Newton method = "REML" works (optimizer = "efs"). Differentially tested pycircstat2: identical models identical data (Newton-REML sides) agree machine precision parametric cyclic-spline fits, optimizer stopping tolerance (~3e-5, fitted curves within 2e-6 radians) thin-plate fits. Tolerances pinned test suite. derivative algebra transcribed pycircstat2’s finite-difference-verified implementations, R-side FD checks link chain assembled gradient/Hessian.
